origin.name: Brazil
origin.investigating_authority: Instituto Medico Legal (IML) and Ministerio Publico
origin.post_mortem_trigger: All violent, sudden, suspicious, or unexplained deaths referred to IML
origin.death_certificate_name: Certidao de obito (from cartorio de registro civil)
origin.iml_note: IML (Instituto Medico Legal) is Brazil's forensic service. Body cannot be released until IML examination is complete and Ministerio Publico issues authorisation.
origin.state_variation_note: Brazil has 26 states and the Federal District, each with its own IML procedures. Sao Paulo and Rio have higher caseloads and sometimes slower processing.
origin.key_document: Certidao de obito with certified English translation and IML release document
origin.source: FCDO Brazil travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Brasilia guidance

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Certidao de obito with certified English translation
special_rules: IML examination is mandatory for all violent, sudden, or unexplained deaths. Cannot be bypassed.
Certidao de obito is in Portuguese. Certified English translation is mandatory.
Brazil's size means timelines vary significantly by state and city.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Brazilian documentation must be in certified English. The coroner for the district is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. British Embassy Brasilia and Consulate General Sao Paulo can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: Brazil to UK Repatriation: Guidance for British Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Brazil. Repatriation to the UK takes 3-6 weeks. IML release, Portuguese documentation, and Sao Paulo cargo flights explained. Contact us 24/7.

destinations.name: Ireland
key_documents: Certidao de obito with certified English translation

airlines.name: LATAM
airlines.routes: GRU to DUB (via LHR or MAD)

special_rules: Irish Embassy in Brasilia handles consular matters.
IML and Ministerio Publico procedures apply identically to Irish repatriations.
destinations.dest_reception: The Irish funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Brazilian documentation must be in certified English. The Coroner is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: Department of Foreign Affairs emergency line: +353 1 408 2000. The Irish Embassy in Brasilia can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: Brazil to Ireland: Funeral Repatriation Guidance
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Brazil and needs to come home to Ireland. Repatriation takes 3-6 weeks. IML release and all documentation handled. Contact us 24/7.
